roadmap_checkpoints.label: Learn a Programming Language
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces you to the fundamentals of a programming language commonly used for infrastructure automation, such as Python or Go. You'll learn essential syntax, data structures, and control flow to write scripts and interact with APIs.
learning_goals: Understand basic programming syntax and constructs.
Write simple scripts to automate repetitive tasks.
Utilize variables, loops, and functions effectively.
Interpret and debug basic programming errors.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Ubuntu / Debian
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Master the administration of Ubuntu and Debian, two widely used Linux distributions in cloud environments. This includes navigating the command line, managing packages, configuring networking, and understanding basic system services.
learning_goals: Perform essential command-line interface (CLI) operations.
Manage software packages using APT.
Configure network interfaces and troubleshoot connectivity.
Understand user and group management, along with file permissions.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Learn a Programming Language + Ubuntu / Debian Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your foundational knowledge in both basic programming concepts and essential Ubuntu/Debian server administration. It covers scripting logic, package management, user administration, and navigating the Linux filesystem.
learning_goals: Demonstrate basic programming syntax and problem-solving skills.
Correctly answer questions on Ubuntu/Debian package management.
Identify and apply common Linux commands for system administration.
Explain the purpose and function of user/group permissions on Linux.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: FreeBSD
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Delve into the FreeBSD operating system, understanding its unique architecture, kernel, and system management tools. This lesson highlights the differences and similarities with Linux, preparing you for diverse server environments.
learning_goals: Install and perform basic configuration of a FreeBSD system.
Manage system services using rc.d scripts.
Understand the FreeBSD Ports and Packages system for software installation.
Differentiate key architectural and operational aspects of FreeBSD from Linux.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: NetBSD
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Explore NetBSD, renowned for its portability and clean design, learning its core principles and system administration. This module helps you understand its use cases, particularly in highly diverse or embedded hardware contexts.
learning_goals: Install and configure a NetBSD system for basic operation.
Utilize pkgsrc for managing software packages.
Understand NetBSD's system layout and configuration files.
Identify the unique advantages and use cases of NetBSD.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Forward Proxy
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Understand the concept and implementation of a forward proxy server. Learn how it functions to route client requests, enhance security, provide anonymity, and implement caching for improved performance.
learning_goals: Explain the role and benefits of a forward proxy in network architecture.
Configure a simple forward proxy server (e.g., Squid).
Understand how proxies contribute to network security and access control.
Troubleshoot common issues related to forward proxy configurations.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Tomcat
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn to install, configure, and manage Apache Tomcat, a popular open-source web server and servlet container used for hosting Java-based web applications. This covers deploying WAR files, configuring connectors, and basic performance tuning.
learning_goals: Install and set up an Apache Tomcat server.
Deploy web applications (WAR files) to Tomcat.
Configure Tomcat connectors and server settings.
Monitor Tomcat logs and perform basic troubleshooting.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: FreeBSD + NetBSD + Forward Proxy + Tomcat Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This comprehensive quiz evaluates your proficiency in managing FreeBSD and NetBSD systems, configuring forward proxies, and administering Tomcat servers. It combines theoretical knowledge with practical application scenarios.
learning_goals: Answer questions distinguishing features and commands of FreeBSD and NetBSD.
Identify appropriate scenarios and configurations for a forward proxy.
Demonstrate knowledge of deploying and troubleshooting applications on Tomcat.
Explain the security and performance implications of these technologies.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: IIS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Gain proficiency in Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS), learning to install, configure, and manage this robust web server platform for hosting ASP.NET and other web applications on Windows servers.
learning_goals: Install and configure IIS on a Windows Server.
Host multiple websites and web applications on IIS.
Manage application pools and worker processes.
Understand basic IIS security features and URL rewriting.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Caching Server
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Explore the crucial role of caching servers in modern infrastructure. Learn how to implement and optimize caching strategies to improve application performance, reduce database load, and enhance user experience.
learning_goals: Explain the principles and benefits of caching.
Implement a caching server (e.g., Redis, Varnish) in a web application stack.
Monitor cache hit/miss ratios and performance metrics.
Understand cache invalidation strategies and their challenges.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Load Balancer
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Understand the vital function of load balancers in distributing network traffic across multiple servers to ensure high availability, scalability, and optimal resource utilization. Learn about different load balancing algorithms and health checks.
learning_goals: Explain the different types of load balancing and their use cases.
Configure a software or hardware load balancer (e.g., Nginx, HAProxy).
Implement health checks for backend servers.
Troubleshoot traffic distribution and server availability issues.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Cloud Infrastructure Engineer Project #1
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This hands-on project challenges you to design and implement a scalable and highly available web application infrastructure. You will integrate a web server, a caching layer, and a load balancer on Linux virtual machines.
learning_goals: Deploy a multi-tier web application stack from scratch.
Configure a load balancer to distribute traffic efficiently.
Integrate a caching server to improve application performance.
Demonstrate basic server hardening and monitoring.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Google Cloud
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Get started with Google Cloud Platform (GCP), one of the leading cloud providers. Learn to provision and manage core services like Compute Engine (VMs), VPC networks, Cloud Storage, and Identity and Access Management (IAM).
learning_goals: Navigate the Google Cloud Console and use gcloud CLI.
Provision and manage virtual machines (Compute Engine).
Configure basic networking resources (VPC, firewalls).
Understand and apply IAM roles for secure access control.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Digital Ocean
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn to provision and manage infrastructure on DigitalOcean, a developer-friendly cloud platform. This covers creating Droplets (VMs), managing managed databases, configuring firewalls, and utilizing object storage (Spaces).
learning_goals: Create and manage Droplets and SSH into them.
Deploy a web application or service on DigitalOcean.
Configure network firewalls and security groups.
Understand DigitalOcean's storage and managed database offerings.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Google Cloud + Digital Ocean Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz tests your fundamental understanding of services and concepts across both Google Cloud Platform and DigitalOcean. It covers VM instantiation, networking basics, storage options, and identity management unique to each platform.
learning_goals: Differentiate core services and pricing models between GCP and DigitalOcean.
Answer questions regarding VM provisioning and management on both platforms.
Explain fundamental networking and firewall concepts in a multi-cloud context.
Identify best practices for identity and access management on each provider.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Hetzner
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Explore Hetzner Cloud, a European cloud provider known for its powerful and cost-effective bare metal and cloud servers. Learn to provision instances, configure networking, and understand its unique service model.
learning_goals: Provision and manage virtual servers on Hetzner Cloud.
Configure networking and firewall rules within Hetzner's ecosystem.
Understand Hetzner's pricing structure and resource allocation.
Utilize Hetzner's panel for server management and monitoring.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Heroku
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn to deploy and manage applications on Heroku, a popular Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS). This includes understanding Git-based deployments, Dynos, buildpacks, environment variables, and the Heroku Add-ons ecosystem.
learning_goals: Successfully deploy an application to Heroku using Git.
Understand Heroku Dynos and the execution environment.
Manage application configuration through environment variables.
Scale Heroku applications and integrate with add-on services.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Contabo
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Get familiar with Contabo's infrastructure services, focusing on their robust dedicated server and VPS offerings. Learn to provision servers, navigate their control panel, and understand their resource scaling options.
learning_goals: Provision and set up a Virtual Private Server (VPS) on Contabo.
Access and utilize the Contabo customer control panel.
Configure server resources and operating system installations.
Understand Contabo's service tiers and hardware specifications.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: FTP / SFTP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Master the File Transfer Protocol (FTP) and its secure counterpart, Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP). Learn how to transfer files between local and remote systems securely, manage permissions, and troubleshoot connectivity.
learning_goals: Use FTP and SFTP clients to transfer files effectively.
Configure an FTP/SFTP server for secure file sharing.
Understand the security implications and best practices for both protocols.
Troubleshoot common connection and authentication issues with FTP/SFTP.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Hetzner + Heroku + Contabo + FTP / SFTP Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz evaluates your knowledge of diverse cloud/hosting providers (Hetzner, Heroku, Contabo) and essential file transfer protocols (FTP/SFTP). It covers deployment models, security, and operational differences.
learning_goals: Compare and contrast the offerings of Hetzner, Heroku, and Contabo.
Answer questions about deploying and managing applications on Heroku.
Explain the security differences and best practices for FTP vs. SFTP.
Troubleshoot common issues related to these providers and protocols.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: HTTP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Deep dive into the Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP), the foundation of data communication for the World Wide Web. Understand HTTP methods, status codes, headers, and the request/response cycle.
learning_goals: Explain the fundamental concepts of HTTP requests and responses.
Identify common HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) and their uses.
Interpret HTTP status codes and their meanings.
Analyze HTTP headers and their role in web communication.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: White / Grey Listing
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn about whitelisting and greylisting, two critical techniques used in network security, particularly for email spam prevention and access control. Understand how they filter unwanted traffic and enhance security posture.
learning_goals: Define whitelisting and its application in security contexts.
Explain greylisting and its effectiveness in combating spam.
Implement whitelisting/greylisting for email servers or network access.
Identify the pros and cons of each technique in different scenarios.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: IMAP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Understand the Internet Message Access Protocol (IMAP) for retrieving and managing email messages directly on a mail server. Learn its features, folder management capabilities, and how it differs from POP3.
learning_goals: Explain the functionality and benefits of IMAP for email access.
Configure an IMAP client to connect to an email server.
Understand how IMAP supports server-side email management (folders, flags).
Differentiate between IMAP and POP3 protocols and their use cases.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Cloud Infrastructure Engineer Project #2
roadmap_checkpoints.description: In this project, you'll apply your knowledge of networking protocols and security to design and deploy a secure and functional email service. You'll configure SMTP, IMAP, and implement spam filtering techniques like whitelisting.
learning_goals: Set up and configure a complete email server (SMTP, IMAP).
Implement and test email authentication and spam filtering using whitelisting.
Demonstrate secure email communication practices.
Troubleshoot common issues related to email delivery and access.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Domain Keys
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn about DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM), an email authentication method designed to detect email spoofing. Understand how DKIM works by cryptographically signing outgoing emails to verify their sender and integrity.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and mechanism of DKIM in email authentication.
Generate and configure DKIM records in DNS.
Verify DKIM signatures for incoming and outgoing emails.
Troubleshoot common DKIM configuration and validation issues.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: OSI Model
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Gain a foundational understanding of the Open Systems Interconnection (OSI) model, a conceptual framework used to describe the functions of a networking system. Learn about its seven layers and how data flows through them.
learning_goals: List and describe all seven layers of the OSI model.
Explain the primary functions performed at each OSI layer.
Map common network protocols and devices to their respective OSI layers.
Use the OSI model as a framework for troubleshooting network issues.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Netlify
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Explore Netlify, a modern web development platform that automates code deployment, continuous integration, and global content delivery. Learn to deploy static sites, use serverless functions, and configure custom domains.
learning_goals: Deploy a static website or single-page application to Netlify.
Configure continuous deployment from a Git repository.
Implement Netlify Functions for serverless backend logic.
Set up custom domains and HTTPS for Netlify hosted sites.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Domain Keys + OSI Model + Netlify Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of critical email authentication (Domain Keys), fundamental network architecture (OSI Model), and modern web deployment practices using Netlify. It covers both theoretical and practical aspects.
learning_goals: Answer questions about DKIM configuration and its role in email security.
Explain the functions of each layer within the OSI model.
Demonstrate knowledge of deploying and managing projects on Netlify.
Identify how these concepts contribute to secure and performant cloud infrastructure.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Secret Management
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Understand the critical importance of secure secret management in cloud infrastructure. Learn best practices and tools for securely storing, accessing, rotating, and auditing sensitive credentials like API keys, passwords, and certificates.
learning_goals: Explain the principles of secure secret management (e.g., least privilege, rotation).
Utilize a secret management solution (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, cloud-native services).
Implement secure practices for injecting secrets into applications and deployments.
Understand the lifecycle of secrets and how to manage their rotation.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Grafana
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Master Grafana, a powerful open-source platform for data visualization and monitoring. Learn to create dynamic dashboards, connect to various data sources, and configure alerts to keep track of your infrastructure's health and performance.
learning_goals: Create and customize Grafana dashboards for infrastructure monitoring.
Connect Grafana to different data sources (e.g., Prometheus, Loki, CloudWatch).
Design effective visualizations using various panel types.
Set up alerting rules and notification channels in Grafana.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Secret Management + Grafana Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz evaluates your understanding of secure secret management principles and your practical ability to use Grafana for monitoring and visualization. It combines security best practices with observability tools.
learning_goals: Answer questions on secure handling and lifecycle management of sensitive data.
Explain core features and use cases of Grafana for infrastructure monitoring.
Identify best practices for designing effective Grafana dashboards.
Troubleshoot common issues in secret access or Grafana data source connectivity.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Docker Swarm
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn to orchestrate Docker containers using Docker Swarm, Docker's native clustering solution. Understand concepts like services, tasks, nodes, and how to deploy and scale applications across a cluster of Docker hosts.
learning_goals: Initialize and manage a Docker Swarm cluster.
Deploy and scale containerized services using Docker Swarm.
Perform rolling updates and rollbacks for Swarm services.
Understand Swarm networking and secrets management.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Nexus
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Explore Nexus Repository Manager, a universal artifact repository that supports various formats like Maven, npm, Docker, and PyPI. Learn to install, configure, and manage repositories for efficient software development and delivery.
learning_goals: Install and set up Nexus Repository Manager.
Configure different types of repositories (proxy, hosted, group).
Upload and download artifacts to and from Nexus.
Integrate Nexus with build tools and CI/CD pipelines.

tasks.title: HTML Elements
tasks.description: Learn the core HTML elements and how to structure a semantic web page.

tasks.title: CSS Styling
tasks.description: Apply styles using selectors, the box model, and Flexbox/Grid layouts.

tasks.title: Javascript Components
tasks.description: Build interactive UI components using vanilla JavaScript and DOM events.

tasks.title: Python Fundamentals
tasks.description: Explore Python syntax, data structures, and scripting basics.

achievements.title: Zari's Movie Binge
achievements.description: Complete 5 lessons in a single day

achievements.title: Eddy's Wood Carving Craft
achievements.description: Finish a project checkpoint from start to finish

cp-003.question: Which property allows a nested grid to align its tracks exactly with those of its parent container?
cp-003.explanation: The grid-template-columns: subgrid; property allows a nested grid to align its tracks with its parent container.

cp-003.question: Which CSS feature is best for changing a component's layout based on the width of its own parent container?
cp-003.explanation: Container Queries are used to change a component's layout based on the width of its own parent container.

cp-003.question: If an element has width: 300px, padding: 20px, and box-sizing: border-box, what is its total rendered width?
cp-003.explanation: With box-sizing: border-box, the padding is included in the width, so the total rendered width remains 300px.

cp-003.question: What is the most semantic HTML5 element for a group of introductory content or navigation links?
cp-003.explanation: The <header> element is specifically designed for introductory content, logos, and navigational aids.

cp-003.question: How can you manage style priority without relying on high-specificity selectors or !important?
cp-003.explanation: Cascade Layers (@layers) allow you to manage style priority without relying on high-specificity selectors or !important.
